Yulang `v0.1.0-alpha.8` is an alpha release that opens the Server Resource
Contract and makes a real TCP server start from one line of Yulang.

Contract v0 stable-core and the Contract v1 file-resource surface remain
closed. This release adds the first executable server slice — tiered host
manifests, suspend one-shot / multi-shot execution with structured child
branches, an in-process mock-server driver, and a native TCP startup slice —
together with parser patterns in `case` arms, a string API v1, and the
nondet line-editing idiom over file refs.

## Highlights

- Declared the `std::io::net` server host act per the io-resource spec:
  `accept` is a suspend multi-shot operation whose every resume spawns a
  structured child branch, and `respond` consumes a one-shot slot returning
  `result () net_err`. Double respond is a typed `net_err::closed` failure.
- Landed suspend-tier execution in the evidence VM: opaque resume tokens,
  scheduler-queue-only resumes (hosts never re-enter the runtime), cancelled
  branches reject late resumes, and multi-shot `close()` rejects later
  resumes.
- The unscoped one-line form works without any import:
  `my req = net::serve 8080`. A prelude glob collision that hijacked the
  `net::` path prefix was fixed along the way.
- Added a native TCP startup slice behind `--host native`: `net::listen`,
  real sockets on `127.0.0.1`, a single-thread nonblocking event pump, and a
  raw-TCP alpha policy (read to client EOF as the request payload; respond
  writes and closes). A real-socket CLI test echoes two connections through
  structured child branches.
- Parser patterns: `~"..."` and `rule { ... }` literals now work as `case`
  arm patterns with full-consumption semantics, capture-record field
  binding, guards, rest capture `..`, and interpolated parser references —
  enough to express request routing in plain `case` arms.
- String API v1: public `find` / `contains` / `starts_with` / `ends_with` /
  `split` / `split_once` / `join` / `trim` family / `repeat` / `to_int`,
  plus mutating methods lifted onto `ref '['e] str` (`replace_once`,
  `replace`, `replace_all`, `splice`, `trim` family) as one-line sugar over
  `ref.update`.
- The nondet line-editing idiom over file refs now runs end to end:
  `(&doc.lines.each.replace_once "todo:" "done:").list` edits every matching
  line of a real file and commits at handler-extent end. Fixing this chain
  closed six pipeline bugs across lowering (effect rows in invariant type
  arguments), inference (record-field fallback firing before constraint
  quiescence), specialize (effect-row width joins), and the evidence VM
  (continuation marker hygiene).
- Parser EOF recovery no longer panics on mid-edit sources such as
  unterminated strings or trailing `::`, with prefix-totality tests; editor
  semantic tokens keep a lexical fallback, and `yulang run` now rejects
  sources with parse diagnostics instead of silently succeeding.
- Hover and playground types are rendered through a public projection:
  solver stack weights display in the user notation (`'c@∅`, `@(∀)`,
  `@[hs]`, `@(except [hs])`) with structural truncation, while debug dumps
  keep the full solver spelling.
- The playground now renders structured diagnostics (label, hint, related
  ranges with origins) for `run`, and the deployed site includes all of the
  above.
- Promoted the new surfaces into the executable contract spine:
  standard-api (string / ref-str), parser patterns, file ambient
  branch-sharing semantics, the new `server-resource` tag and evidence
  documents, and the run parse-diagnostics gate.

## Install

Unix-like systems:

```sh
curl -fsSL https://yulang.momota.pw/install.sh | sh -s -- --version v0.1.0-alpha.8
```

Windows PowerShell:

```powershell
Invoke-WebRequest https://yulang.momota.pw/install.ps1 -OutFile install.ps1
powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\install.ps1 -Version v0.1.0-alpha.8
```

## Known Limitations

- The language and standard library remain alpha-stage and may still change.
- The server contract covers the mock-server first slice plus the native TCP
  startup slice. The raw-TCP policy (read-to-EOF payload, respond-then-close)
  is explicitly unstable pre-HTTP-adapter; natural termination, timeouts,
  backpressure, cancellation surface, and the connect side are not yet
  contracted. Native servers run until killed.
- HTTP adapters, `l.requests` / `serve_with`, and Yumark content delivery are
  the next tracks on top of this slice, not part of this alpha.
- Grapheme-cluster iteration, case conversion, and `to_float` are deferred to
  a string API v2 that needs new intrinsics.
- The evidence VM is the default runtime path, but the control VM remains
  available as a fallback/debug path while runtime hardening continues.
